batch_metadata.note: Phase B/C transition. Sep-Oct 2025. Three-block data structure. Oct 4-7 'so afraid of you' child-fear relay cluster (COMP-RF04-026, RF-04+RF-05). Sep 17 'agreement ready to sign' RF-05 peak event. Contains S-006: ROW-14499+14508 custodial-time contact during Eli's parenting time.

flagged_messages.message_text: ROW-14499 (Received): 'you better not stop her from contacting me next time...I'm still contemplating contempt charges' | ROW-14508 (Received): 'She doesn't feel safe and supported as evidenced by the texts I got' | ROW-14517 (Received): 'the feedback I got in text the last weekend she was with you was very alarming. Also the conversation I had with her while she was crying in her room and begging me not to say anything to you'
flagged_messages.primary_reasoning: Custodial-time contact overstepping per ADD-004. Oct 4-7 cluster: Melissa contacts Eli during his parenting weekend with messages referencing Olivia's texts to Melissa 'the last weekend she was with you' — deploying Olivia's contact with Melissa during Eli's parenting time as evidence against Eli ('She doesn't feel safe and supported as evidenced by the texts I got'). Mechanism: (1) Melissa maintained contact with Olivia during Eli's parenting time; (2) Olivia's distress signals during Eli's time were captured and weaponized as evidence; (3) ROW-14499 adds a contempt threat for interfering with Olivia's contact with Melissa. ADD-004: contact during Eli's parenting time for non-urgent reasons (character attack, legal threats) constitutes custodial-time overstepping. Content is not emergency logistics — it is retrospective character attack using child-mediated evidence. ROW-14517: 'crying in her room and begging me not to say anything to you' — this communication occurred during Eli's parenting time. Sev-1: cluster context; contempt threat + child-mediated concern. Sev-2 would require explicit response-demand ('why aren't you answering?') during identified parenting time. ARC-RF07-001-S006.

flagged_messages.calibration_notes: Sev-1: custodial-time overstepping confirmed by ADD-004 mechanism (Melissa maintaining Olivia contact during Eli's parenting time and weaponizing that contact as evidence). Compound with COMP-RF04-026 (RF-04 child-fear relay primary). RF-07 custodial-time-overstepping is the ADD-004 mechanism; the RF-04 character-attack content is the compound component.

arc_state_snapshot.note: ARC-RF07-001 ACTIVE. S-006 added (ROW-14499+14508, custodial-time overstepping, Sev-1). Arc now at 6 signals. Sub-pattern: custodial_time_contact_overstepping (ADD-004) confirmed as arc component alongside flooding_candidate, response_demand, continuation_after_self_demand, read_receipt_pressure.

batch_observations.tone_assessment: High escalation — Aug 28-Oct 16 2025. Sep 17 'agreement ready to sign' RF-05 peak. Aug 28-29 Labor Day visit refusal. Oct 4-7 'so afraid of you' child-fear relay. The dataset's most severe RF-04+RF-05 compound periods cluster in this batch.
batch_observations.rf07_assessment: One primary signal (Sev-1 custodial-time overstepping). The Oct 4-7 cluster documents Melissa's contact with Olivia during Eli's parenting time and deployment of that contact as character-attack evidence — ADD-004 mechanism confirmed. The 'so afraid of you' framing, while primarily RF-04+RF-05, is delivered through custodial-time contact, making it RF-07 as well.
batch_observations.custodial_context: S-006 directly addresses custodial-time contact: Melissa was in contact with Olivia during Eli's parenting weekend, and that contact was then weaponized in messages to Eli. ADD-004 confirmed: Melissa's contact with Olivia during Eli's parenting time served non-urgent purpose (character documentation and legal threat) rather than genuine safety need.
